title: ilex crenata f. fastigiata
intro: Ilex crenata f. fastigiata, also known as Japanese holly, is a compact, evergreen shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its glossy, dark green leaves and bright red berries. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Ilex crenata f. fastigiata include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and act as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the leaves and berries of this plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
